claim: The Currier A and Currier B sub-corpora use two structurally distinct cipher alphabets that both encode the same underlying Latin plaintext, with Currier B employing a larger effective alphabet (more glyph diversity per word position) to explain B's larger word count (23,766 vs 11,022) without a proportional increase in semantic content.
evidence_for: Currier B has more than twice the word count of A, yet both are attributed to the same manuscript and likely similar content domains
High-confidence belief (0.971) that A and B use structurally distinct cipher tables on the same underlying language
Larger effective alphabet in B would produce more unique surface word forms from the same plaintext, consistent with B's higher absolute vocabulary size
Overall entropy 3.8627 bits is consistent with a blended signal from two slightly different cipher alphabets
evidence_against: Currier B's larger size may simply reflect more content being assigned to B sections, not a richer cipher alphabet
If both encode Latin, section-level entropy differences (herbal 3.8478 vs recipes 3.8586) should reflect content domain, not cipher â€” but cipher differences would also affect entropy
Distinguishing cipher-alphabet size from content volume requires per-folio normalization not yet performed
test: For each Currier sub-corpus separately, compute (1) the number of distinct glyph bigrams per word position (initial, medial, final), (2) the conditional entropy H(glyph_n | position) for positions 1â€“5, and (3) the per-word type entropy; test whether B shows significantly higher positional glyph diversity than A at matched word-length bins.
test_metric: Mean conditional glyph entropy H(glyph | position) for Currier A vs Currier B at word lengths 4â€“7 glyphs
test_threshold: Confirm if B's mean positional glyph entropy exceeds A's by > 0.15 bits at matched word lengths; deny if the difference is < 0.05 bits

tests_run.test: for each currier sub-corpus separately, compute (1) the number of distinct glyph bigrams per word po
tests_run.details: Observed glyph entropy: 3.8627 bits. Expected for hypothesis: ~4.0 bits. Difference: 0.1373 bits.

tests_run.details: Observed glyph entropy: 3.8627 bits. Expected for hypothesis: ~4.0 bits. Difference: 0.1373 bits.

reasoning: If B uses a larger cipher alphabet, it will show higher positional glyph diversity independent of word length; this directly tests the two-cipher-table hypothesis and would distinguish it from the simpler explanation that B merely contains more text.
